title: Users unable to log in to portal
description: Multiple users reporting they cannot log in to the customer portal. Browser shows a 401 Unauthorized error after entering correct credentials. Issue started around 09:15 this morning. Approximately 30 users affected across the London office.
resolution_notes: Restarted auth service on node auth-prod-02. Sessions were being invalidated due to a token expiry misconfiguration. Users were able to log in after service restart.

title: Login page showing 'Session expired' error repeatedly
description: Users are getting 'Your session has expired, please log in again' messages immediately after logging in. The error appears within seconds of a successful login. Affects all users trying to access the portal from the Chicago office.
resolution_notes: Cleared session cache on auth service. Root cause appears to be token TTL set too low in last deployment. Temporary fix applied — permanent configuration change needed.

title: Authentication failure for entire customer support team
description: The entire customer support team (approximately 45 users) cannot authenticate to any internal tools. SSO is failing with error code AUTH-4012. The helpdesk has been overwhelmed with calls. Started at 08:00 this morning.
resolution_notes: Auth service was restarted. Investigation showed that JWT token signing keys were rotated automatically but the rotation was not propagated to all service instances. Re-seeded the token store.

title: Can't access portal after password change
description: User reports that after successfully changing their password via the self-service portal, they are unable to log back in. The system says their session is invalid. Other users on the same team can log in fine.
resolution_notes: User had to re-authenticate after we manually invalidated their old session token. The issue is that password changes do not properly invalidate existing sessions, leaving the user in a broken state.

title: Session keeps expiring during active work
description: Multiple power users complain that their sessions expire while they are actively working. They are mid-task and suddenly get redirected to the login page. This is happening every 30-45 minutes during active sessions, which is disrupting workflows significantly.
resolution_notes: Identified that the session idle timeout is being applied incorrectly — it is counting from session creation rather than last activity. Workaround: users must log in again. Permanent fix requires auth service code change.

title: Portal login broken after maintenance window
description: Following last night's scheduled maintenance window, approximately 200 users cannot log in to the portal this morning. Auth service appears to be running but returning 401 for all login attempts. Error in logs: 'Token validation failed: key not found'.
resolution_notes: Maintenance window included a key rotation that was not reflected in the auth service configuration. Rolled back the key configuration and service came back online. Users had to re-authenticate.

title: Intermittent 401 errors on API gateway for authenticated users
description: Authenticated users are sporadically receiving 401 errors when calling the internal API gateway. The errors occur about 1 in 20 requests. Users must refresh their token to resolve temporarily. Issue seems worse under load.
resolution_notes: Traced to race condition in token refresh logic — under load, tokens expire between validation and use. Workaround: increase token TTL. Long-term fix: implement token refresh with mutex lock.

title: Users logged out automatically every hour
description: All users across the platform are being automatically logged out after exactly 60 minutes, even if they are actively using the system. This started after the v2.3.1 deployment on Monday. Very disruptive for users in the middle of long-running tasks.
resolution_notes: v2.3.1 introduced a hardcoded 3600-second session TTL that overrides the configurable value. Patched and redeployed. Sessions now persist as expected.

title: New employees cannot log in on first day
description: A batch of 12 new starters provisioned this morning cannot log in to any systems. They receive 'Invalid credentials' despite having correct temporary passwords. IT onboarding is blocked.
resolution_notes: The provisioning script creates accounts but the auth service token store was not being updated for new accounts. Manually triggered a sync and accounts became available. Auth service and provisioning pipeline need integration fix.

title: Two-factor authentication codes not working
description: Users with 2FA enabled are reporting that their authenticator app codes are being rejected even when entered within the valid time window. Approximately 80 users affected. The error message says 'Authentication code expired or invalid'.
resolution_notes: Auth service server time drifted by 90 seconds due to NTP misconfiguration. TOTP codes depend on synchronized time. NTP corrected, server time synced, issue resolved.

title: Mobile app showing 'token invalid' after update
description: Following the mobile app v4.2 release, users are receiving 'Token invalid or expired' when opening the app. They can resolve it by logging out and back in, but the issue recurs every time they close and reopen the app.
resolution_notes: Mobile app v4.2 introduced a new token storage mechanism that is incompatible with the existing token format from the auth service. The app is treating all stored tokens as expired. Rollback to v4.1 issued for affected users.

title: SSO redirect loop prevents login
description: A subset of users (those with accounts in both the legacy and new identity provider) are stuck in a redirect loop when attempting to log in via SSO. They are redirected back and forth between the IdP and the portal without ever being authenticated. Clears only after browser cache is wiped.
resolution_notes: The auth service is issuing a new session token on each redirect without checking for an existing valid token. This creates a loop. Cleared the affected user sessions manually. Fix requires auth service logic update.

title: Monthly compliance report taking 45 minutes to generate
description: The monthly compliance report that normally takes 8-10 minutes is now taking 45 minutes and occasionally timing out before completion. Finance team cannot submit their end-of-month reporting on time. Report is blank when it does eventually load.
resolution_notes: User was advised to wait for the report to complete. Added extra timeout headroom on the report endpoint. Underlying query performance issue not addressed.

title: Dashboard timeout when loading analytics for Q1
description: The analytics dashboard times out with a 504 Gateway Timeout when users try to load Q1 data. The issue occurs consistently when selecting any date range longer than 30 days. Refreshing the page and retrying does not help.
resolution_notes: User eventually got partial data by narrowing date range. Increased query timeout to 120 seconds as a temporary measure. Full Q1 analytics still not loadable.

title: Analytics export failing with 'query timeout' error
description: Attempts to export analytics data to CSV or Excel are failing with 'Query execution exceeded maximum allowed time' error. The export was working fine two weeks ago. Now it fails for any dataset larger than 5,000 rows.
resolution_notes: Refreshed materialized views on the reporting database. Export worked after view refresh. Views need to be refreshed on a schedule but the scheduled job has stopped running.

title: Reports not loading — blank page after spinner
description: Several report types show a loading spinner for approximately 30 seconds then display a blank page with no error message. Affects Sales Summary, Regional Breakdown, and Customer Acquisition reports. Started after last week's database maintenance.
resolution_notes: Reports were waiting for query results that never arrived due to a stale connection in the connection pool. Restarted the reporting service to flush the pool. Reports loaded normally after restart.

title: End-of-day report generation queue backed up
description: The automated end-of-day report generation queue has 47 reports waiting that have not processed since 18:00. Daily business reports are not available for morning review. The reporting service appears to be running but is unresponsive to new jobs.
resolution_notes: Reporting service worker threads were exhausted due to a long-running report query that held all connection pool slots. Killed the stuck query and reports processed within 20 minutes.

title: Performance reports showing partial data only
description: The weekly performance report is showing data for only 60% of the expected time range. The most recent 3 days of data are missing. Users believe reports are complete but are actually making decisions on incomplete information. Silent failure.
resolution_notes: A slow ETL pipeline is causing the report to query before all data is loaded. Report appears to complete but returns partial data. Workaround: add a 2-hour delay before generating weekly reports. Root cause in ETL latency not addressed.

title: Large dataset exports cause reporting service to freeze
description: When users attempt to export datasets larger than 50,000 rows, the reporting service becomes unresponsive for all other users for 5-10 minutes. Other report requests queue up and timeout during this period. Essentially a single large export blocks the entire service.
resolution_notes: Moved large exports to a separate async queue to prevent blocking. Immediate issue mitigated. Long-term fix requires dedicated export worker pool and query resource limits.

title: Report scheduler running all jobs simultaneously causing slowness
description: The nightly report scheduler is triggering all 23 scheduled reports at the same time at midnight instead of staggering them. This causes severe performance degradation and most reports fail or produce partial results.
resolution_notes: Identified that a cron job configuration change removed the staggered start times. Reconfigured to run reports at 5-minute intervals. Reports now complete successfully.

title: Not receiving email notifications for ticket updates
description: Support agents are not receiving email notifications when their assigned tickets are updated by customers. They are missing customer replies and SLA breach warnings. Issue started approximately 3 days ago. Service desk manager raised the issue after noticing increased response times.
resolution_notes: Emails eventually started arriving after approximately 4 hours. No action taken — monitoring the situation.

title: Password reset email not arriving
description: A user locked out of their account requested a password reset email three times over two hours but never received it. They are completely blocked from accessing any company systems. IT support had to manually reset the password as a workaround.
resolution_notes: Manually reset password for user. Email was eventually delivered 5 hours after first request. Notification service email queue appears to have significant latency.

title: Notification emails delayed by 3-6 hours
description: Multiple teams are reporting that notification emails (incident alerts, approval requests, status updates) are arriving 3 to 6 hours after the triggering event. In some cases, by the time the email arrives the situation has already been resolved or escalated without the intended recipient's input.
resolution_notes: Resent affected notifications manually. Email provider confirmed no issues on their end. The delay appears to be within the notification service itself — messages are queued but not being processed promptly.

title: Alert emails for P1 incidents arriving hours late
description: On-call engineers did not receive the P1 incident alert email for the database outage last night. They only became aware of the incident when a customer called. The alert was sent at 02:15 but the engineers did not receive the email until 06:30. Four hours of unnecessary downtime resulted.
resolution_notes: Alert email was found stuck in the notification queue. Resent manually. Post-incident review initiated. P1 alerts should never be delayed — the notification service queueing behaviour needs urgent investigation.

title: Bulk notification send causing email queue backup
description: After sending a bulk product update notification to 5,000 customers, all transactional email notifications (password resets, alerts, approval requests) were delayed for approximately 6 hours. The bulk send appears to have saturated the email processing queue.
resolution_notes: Emails eventually delivered after queue drained. No rate limiting on bulk sends currently exists. Bulk and transactional email should be on separate queues.

title: Approval request emails not being sent
description: Several change approval requests were submitted but the designated approvers never received the notification emails. One change was approved without the required sign-off because the approver was never notified. Compliance audit flagged this.
resolution_notes: Approval notifications were found in a failed state in the notification service. Service had silently failed to deliver them without retrying. Notifications resent manually. Compliance team notified.

title: Weekly digest emails not delivered
description: The weekly summary digest that is sent every Monday morning to all managers was not received this week. 120 managers are affected. The emails were scheduled and triggered but none were delivered.
resolution_notes: Emails eventually arrived on Tuesday morning — approximately 20 hours late. The notification service had throttled email sending due to hitting the daily send limit on Saturday due to a misfired bulk send.

title: One-time data import failure — customer records
description: The quarterly customer data import from the CRM failed partway through, importing only 3,400 of the expected 12,000 records. The import script stopped with an error: 'Duplicate key constraint violation on customer_id'. The import has never failed before.
resolution_notes: Discovered that 8,600 customers had been pre-loaded by another team as part of a data migration. The duplicate check was not robust enough. Wrote a deduplication script and re-ran import successfully.

title: Network outage — Manchester office
description: Complete network outage affecting the Manchester office. All 85 staff cannot access any company systems. The ISP has been contacted and confirmed a fibre cut in the local exchange. No ETA on restoration at this time.
resolution_notes: ISP restored connectivity after 4 hours. Failover to 4G backup router was not configured — this is a gap to address. All systems came back online once network connectivity was restored.

title: Third-party mapping API rate limit reached
description: The customer-facing store locator feature is broken because the Google Maps API rate limit was exceeded. The feature is returning 'REQUEST_DENIED' errors. This happened mid-afternoon on a Tuesday — unusually high traffic day due to a marketing campaign.
resolution_notes: Switched to the backup mapping API key. Added rate limit monitoring alert. Marketing team to notify IT in advance of campaigns that could spike API usage.

title: Disk space warning on archive server
description: The archive storage server (arch-prod-01) is at 92% disk capacity. Automatic alerts triggered. No immediate impact on operations but the server hosts 18 months of log archives. If disk fills, log ingestion will fail.
resolution_notes: Deleted log archives older than 18 months as per retention policy. Disk usage reduced to 67%. Scheduled a quarterly review of archive disk usage.

title: User permission misconfiguration — read access to wrong project
description: A contractor was given access to Project Alpha data instead of Project Beta due to a permissions misconfiguration in the identity management system. They had read access for approximately 6 hours before the error was discovered during a routine access review.
resolution_notes: Access revoked immediately. Contractor confirmed they did not access any Project Alpha files during the window. Root cause: manual entry error in the permissions provisioning form. Checklist process to be implemented.

title: DNS resolution failure — internal service mesh
description: Internal DNS lookups for the service mesh were failing for approximately 25 minutes, causing service-to-service communication to break down across the platform. Automated monitoring caught the issue. Root cause was a CoreDNS pod crash on the Kubernetes cluster.
resolution_notes: Kubernetes automatically restarted the CoreDNS pod within 3 minutes. Full DNS resolution restored after the pod stabilised. Added an additional CoreDNS replica to prevent single point of failure.

title: SSL certificate renewal reminder — payments subdomain
description: SSL certificate for payments.internal.company.com expires in 14 days. Auto-renewal has not triggered. Certificate must be renewed manually or the payment processing subdomain will become inaccessible to users and present security warnings.
resolution_notes: Certificate manually renewed and deployed. Auto-renewal was failing silently because the renewal service account lacked permissions to write the updated certificate. Permissions corrected and auto-renewal re-enabled and tested.

title: Scheduled data warehouse ETL job ran 4 hours late
description: The nightly data warehouse ETL job that usually completes by 05:00 did not finish until 09:15, causing the morning analytics dashboard to show stale data for the first business hours of the day. Two executives were already in meetings presenting potentially outdated figures.
resolution_notes: The ETL job was delayed because it was waiting on a lock held by an ad-hoc query run by a data analyst at 01:00. The analyst's query ran for 8 hours before completing. Lock contention rules to be implemented for nightly jobs.
